title: carex harlandii subsp. harlandii
intro: Carex harlandii subsp. harlandii is a subspecies of sedge, a type of perennial grass-like plant native to Asia, characterized by its narrow, pointed leaves and brownish-green seed stalks. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, with uses including treating fever, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Carex harlandii subsp. harlandii are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Carex harlandii subsp. harlandii are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to alleviate respiratory issues, improve digestion, and support immune function.
